1. A 45-year-old man with a history of poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with a painless foot ulcer. On exam, the ulcer is located on the plantar surface of his right foot. He has decreased sensation to monofilament testing bilaterally. What is the most likely underlying mechanism?
A. Peripheral arterial disease
B. Peripheral neuropathy
C. Venous insufficiency
D. Deep vein thrombosis
E. Osteomyelitis

2. A 70-year-old woman presents with acute onset confusion, fever, and a new heart murmur. Blood cultures grow Streptococcus viridans. Echocardiography shows a vegetation on the mitral valve. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment?
A. Vancomycin IV for 2 weeks
B. Penicillin G IV for 4 weeks
C. Oral amoxicillin for 10 days
D. Ceftriaxone IM for 2 weeks
E. Watchful waiting with repeat echocardiogram

Answers:
1. B
2. B
